evals.prompt: Design a REST API for a blog platform. It needs endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting blog posts. Posts have a title, body, author, tags, and published status. Include authentication.
evals.expected_output: A complete OpenAPI 3.1 YAML specification with paths for CRUD operations on posts, request/response schemas, authentication scheme, and standard error responses.
expectations: Output is valid YAML starting with 'openapi: "3.1.0"'
Spec includes info, servers, and paths sections
At least 4 HTTP methods defined (GET list, GET by id, POST, PATCH or PUT, DELETE)
Each endpoint has at least a 2xx response and one error response (4xx)
components/schemas includes at least a Post schema and a request body schema
Security scheme is defined (bearerAuth or apiKeyAuth)
Pagination parameters present on the list endpoint
Error response uses a consistent ErrorResponse schema referenced via $ref

evals.prompt: Design an API for a task management app. Users can create projects, add tasks to projects, assign tasks to team members, and update task status. Use OpenAPI.
evals.expected_output: OpenAPI YAML with separate resources for projects and tasks, with nested relationships, assignment endpoint, and status update endpoint.
expectations: Output is valid YAML with openapi field
Task assignment operation is defined (patch or post to an assignment endpoint)
Task status values are enumerated in the schema
Response schemas for both Project and Task are defined in components/schemas
All path parameters have schema definitions
